prose:
Method–provenance summary (all required fields)
Property: molecular dipole moment magnitude (Debye)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (Grimme’s self-consistent tight-binding, no Gaussian basis)
Geometry provenance: fixed geometry from user-supplied /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/electrostatics-validation/water/water.xyz; no optimization was performed (single-point electrostatics).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model applied)
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software/backend: xtb through chemkit electrostatics skill (version field not returned by engine)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged; integrity.status = ok; no warnings reported; partial charges sum to –7.2×10⁻¹⁶ e, satisfying charge-consistency check.

Reproducibility artifacts
• Result JSON written to /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmp8n9dwp2n/live.json (contains cli_invocation and full output).
• A live engine log (.out) was streamed during the run; the file path is given in the engine’s header and can be tailed for full SCF details.

Headline result
Dipole moment (|μ|) = 2.2870277 Debye  (GFN2-xTB, gas phase, water single-point).

No experimental comparison is offered here (computed value only).
